channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US court rules that Trump’s name must stay off Kennedy Center during appeal
item.description: Trump’s name was removed from the centre's facade and signage last month, after a judge ordered its removal.

item.title: Do fans still trust referees and VAR at the World Cup?
item.description: Do fans still trust referees and VAR at the World Cup?

item.title: What’s with Trump’s sudden fixation on communism?
item.description: US President Donald Trump has denounced communism more than 80 times in the past two weeks.

item.title: IMF cuts 2026 world growth forecast, citing Iran war fallout
item.description: Global economy to grow 3 percent in 2026, as AI demand partly offsets energy shock from Iran war, IMF says.

item.title: Would the US protect NATO allies if they were attacked?
item.description: This is America: Jim Townsend says he is unsure whether the US would send troops if a NATO ally were attacked.

item.title: US Senate nominee Platner halts campaign after assault allegations
item.description: A string of high-profile Democrats have withdrawn support for Platner following sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Confirmed Ebola deaths in DR Congo hit 600
item.description: The number of deaths comes as healthcare workers threaten to walk off the job over a delay in payments.

item.title: Trump to ask US Supreme Court for new hearing on birthright citizenship
item.description: In June, the Supreme Court ruled against Trump’s order to end birthright citizenship for individuals born in the US.

item.title: Rights group plans complaint to IOC against Infantino over Trump support
item.description: FairSquare says it will lodge the complaint regarding the FIFA president's 'repeated breach of political neutrality'.

item.title: Morocco dealt huge blow as injured Saibari out of World Cup quarterfinal
item.description: Ismael Saibari will miss the match against France in Boston due to a hamstring injury sustained last week.

item.title: Eight killed as landslide hits Rohingya school in Bangladesh
item.description: At least seven children and a teacher were killed after a landslide hit a girls’ school at a refugee camp in Bangladesh.

item.title: South Sudan, 15 years on: Still fighting for peace
item.description: A stalled peace process, deepening humanitarian needs and economic challenges test the world's youngest nation.

item.title: Tehran releases footage showing ruins of late Iranian leader’s compound
item.description: Iranian state media has released what it calls the first footage showing damage to the late Khamenei’s compound.

item.title: Khamenei’s coffin in Karbala on last stop before burial in Mashhad
item.description: Millions gathered in the holy city of Karbala, Iraq to pay their respects at the funeral of Iran’s late supreme leader.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait after deadly US raids
item.description: US attacks kill at least one person in the city of Iranshahr and cause widespread power outages in Chabahar.

item.title: Indonesia’s free meals programme plagued by alleged corruption and waste
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's initiative has been criticised for misdirecting resources to affluent regions.

item.title: Wildcard Fery faces Zverev in Wimbledon semis, as Kostyuk meets Noskova
item.description: A partisan Centre Court backed home hope and world number 114 Fery in his incredible fairytale run to the last four.

item.title: US expands military strikes on Iran after Trump says he’ll ‘hit them hard’
item.description: US forces have expanded their military strikes on Iran, after blaming Tehran for breaking the MoU to end the war.

item.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira, BTS in World Cup final halftime show
item.description: FIFA expects 'a couple of billion' to tune in to the 11-minute performance, the first at a World Cup.

item.title: Family demands investigation after US man killed by ICE agent in Texas
item.description: Calls for answers after Lorenzo Salgado Araujo fatally shot during traffic stop in Houston in latest ICE killing.

item.title: US moves to delist Syria as a ‘state sponsor of terrorism’
item.description: President Trump announces a move to delist Syria, citing efforts by Ahmed al-Sharaa to unify the country.

item.title: Former Wisconsin judge avoids prison in ICE obstruction case
item.description: Hannah Dugan's sentence comes after she helped an immigrant in the US avoid ICE agents waiting to arrest him in 2025.

item.title: France lose appeal against Olise’s yellow card in Paraguay World Cup clash
item.description: If Olise is cautioned again against Morocco, he will be suspended for a potential semifinal against Spain or Belgium.

item.title: Deadly strikes in Gaza leave 5 dead, children wounded
item.description: Israeli strikes in Khan Yunis and Gaza City killed five people and wounded others, including children.

item.title: Former rebel turned government ally proposes a path to peace for Sudan
item.description: Malik Agar Ayyir has proposed a national dialogue to end the civil war and steer Sudan towards democratic civilian rule.
